description:
A junior security auditor has scanned file-system permissions and
  stored the raw output in

      /home/user/security_scan/scan_results.log

  Each line of that log follows the exact pattern

      <ABSOLUTE_PATH> <PERM_STRING> <OWNER>

  where
    • <ABSOLUTE_PATH>  is the full path to the file or directory  
    • <PERM_STRING>    is the nine-character symbolic permission string
                         (e.g. “-rw-rw-r--”)  
    • <OWNER>          is the Unix username that owns the object  

  Example line:
      /etc/ssh/ssh_config -rw-r--r-- root

  Your task: prepare a concise summary that lists ONLY the objects which
  are world-writable, i.e. whose permission string shows the letter “w”
  in the “others” field (position 9).

  Requirements
  1. Read /home/user/security_scan/scan_results.log.
  2. Extract every line where character 9 of the permission string is “w”.
  3. Write the matching lines, unchanged, in the same order, to a new
     file named
        /home/user/security_scan/insecure_files_summary.txt
  4. If there are no matches, the summary file must contain exactly the
     single line:
        No insecure files found
  5. Do not add header lines, footers, extra blank lines, or additional
     whitespace.

  Hints (not exhaustive): a combination of grep/awk/cut or similar
  utilities will suffice; expect to need no more than 2-3 commands.

  When you have finished, you can inspect the created summary file to
  verify its contents.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:
    Path: /home/user/security_scan/scan_results.log
    Contents (7 lines, LF-terminated):

/var/www/html/index.php -rw-r--r-- root
/etc/passwd -rw-r--r-- root
/tmp/tempfile.sh -rwxrwxrwx user
/home/user/public_notes.txt -rw-rw-r-- user
/var/log/app.log -rw-r----- syslog
/home/user/scripts/run.sh -rwxr-xr-x user
/opt/software/config.cfg -rw-rw-rw- user

  Expected file the agent must create:
    Path: /home/user/security_scan/insecure_files_summary.txt
    Permissions: default user umask (file just needs to be creatable)
    Expected contents (exactly 2 lines, LF-terminated):

/tmp/tempfile.sh -rwxrwxrwx user
/opt/software/config.cfg -rw-rw-rw- user
name: task_000000_7ef361b5
